title: Desktop Skills Workshop
description: Hands-on desktop automation — open apps, type, save files, and navigate the file system.

tasks.title: Open a Text Editor
tasks.sonic_prompt: Let me show you how to open a text editor on the Linux desktop.

subtasks.title: Open Mousepad
subtasks.sonic_prompt: First, I will open the Mousepad text editor.
subtasks.goal: Open the Mousepad text editor. Right-click on the desktop and look for 'Open Terminal Here', or find Mousepad in the application menu. Alternatively, open a terminal and run 'mousepad' command.

tasks.title: Type and Save a File
tasks.sonic_prompt: Now I will type some text and save it as a file.

subtasks.title: Type text in the editor
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Let me type a message in the text editor.
subtasks.goal: Click on the text area in the Mousepad editor and type 'Hello from Nexora AI! This file was created during the Desktop Skills Workshop.'

subtasks.title: Save the file
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Now I will save this file.
subtasks.goal: Press Ctrl + S to open the Save dialog. Select Desktop from the left panel as the save location, enter nexora_demo.txt as the filename, and click Save.

tasks.title: Verify the File in Terminal
tasks.sonic_prompt: Let me open a terminal and verify that our file was saved correctly.

subtasks.title: Open Terminal
subtasks.sonic_prompt: First, I will open a terminal window.
subtasks.goal: Open the XFCE terminal. Look for the terminal icon in the taskbar at the bottom of the screen, or right-click on the desktop and select 'Open Terminal Here'.

subtasks.title: Read the saved file
subtasks.sonic_prompt: Now I will display the contents of the file we just saved.
subtasks.goal: If a terminal is already open, press Ctrl + Z to return to the terminal prompt. Since the file was saved on the Desktop, navigate to the Desktop directory. Then run cat nexora_demo.txt and press Enter to view the contents of the file.
